title: Rutaea floribunda
taxonomy: Rutaea floribunda is a flowering plant and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Rutaceae, which is a family of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family, and is native to South Africa. The Rutaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies and tribes, with Rutaea floribunda likely being part of the Rutaeae tribe. However, the exact classification of Rutaea floribunda may require further research. It is a small tree or shrub with fragrant flowers and is often used in traditional medicine in its native region. Further study is necessary to fully understand its classification within the Rutaceae family.

intro: Rutaea floribunda, also known as Rutaceae, is a plant species within the family Rutaceae, commonly referred to as the citrus or rue family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Rutaea floribunda include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Rutaea floribunda is traditionally used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, as well as being incorporated into traditional remedies, such as salves and ointments.
